id: "13.7.3"
title: "Fluentd / Fluent Bit Input-to-Output Throughput Mismatch and Per-Pipeline Loss Detection (emit_count versus write_count per tag, silent drops, filter chomping, parser failures, retry starvation, rewrite_tag misroutes, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes parity)"

---

# UC-13.7.3 · Fluentd / Fluent Bit Input-to-Output Throughput Mismatch and Per-Pipeline Loss Detection (emit_count versus write_count per tag, silent drops, filter chomping, parser failures, retry starvation, rewrite_tag misroutes, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes parity)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Picture a busy post office where one hundred letters are accepted at the front desk every minute, yet only ninety are loaded onto the truck labeled for your city. We count what arrives and what actually departs for each route so a gap cannot hide for long, then we point crews to the sorting room, the relabeling belt, or the loading dock that ate the difference.*

---

## Description

Detects sustained divergence between Fluentd ingress counters and egress counters per pipeline tag and output_id by fusing Prometheus-style input totals, output emit totals, Fluent Bit input and output processing counters, parser and filter stage rates, optional engine log evidence for silent drops and regex misses, buffer queue decoupling hints, then computing five-minute throughput parity, parser-adjacent drop mass, filter chomp percentage, and staleness since last matching activity with inventory and CMDB enrichment.

## Value

Reliability and audit stakeholders receive early warning when log volume enters a forwarder but fewer structured events reach Splunk, Elasticsearch, or Kafka sinks, which protects completeness commitments and prevents silent blind spots during parser churn, label rewrites, or Kubernetes metadata changes. Platform teams isolate whether loss sits in filters, parsers, tag routing, memory limits, or decoupled buffers before customer dashboards go quiet.

## Implementation

Land Prometheus exporter metrics into metrics indexes with host, tag, output_id, and plugin dimensions; ship fluentd:logs and fluent_bit:logs; maintain fluentd_pipeline_tag_inventory.csv keyed on tag; schedule observability_uc_13_7_3_fluent_throughput_parity every five minutes with earliest=-4h@h; join cmdb fluentd_pipeline_metadata on tag; route critical rows to platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence CSV snapshots.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_7_3_fluent_throughput_parity with five minute schedule; platform logging dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; alert routing to Head of Platform delegated on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab namespace, introduce a parser regex mismatch that drops ninety percent of events for a single tag while metrics scrapes continue, confirm multisearch arms show rising throughput_diff_pct with elevated parser_drop_count_5min within two five-minute windows, and verify observability_uc_13_7_3_fluent_throughput_parity returns critical with non-null recommended_action tied to that tag.

### Negative scenario

After restoring a compatible parser and allowing input and output counters to realign with quiet warn lines for the tag, confirm severities return to low and filtering to critical plus high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive scheduled runs under calibrated thresholds.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability pipeline engineering, Kubernetes platform teams, and the service owners who contract each logging route. This use case isolates throughput parity between ingress and egress legs of Fluentd, Fluent Bit, Treasure Data td-agent, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes, and Calyptia distributions so silent loss surfaces before disk buffers scream. UC-13.7.1 remains the buffer overflow and retry exhaustion narrative where queue length and retry counters lead. UC-13.7.2 remains the per-destination HTTP error class and retryable versus fatal classification story. UC-13.7.4 remains worker crash and supervisor respawn detection. UC-13.7.5 remains proactive buffer fill slope forecasting. UC-13.7.9 remains TLS trust and handshake failure analytics. This card answers a different question: for a given tag or pipeline label, do accepted records and emitted records stay within expected parity, and when they diverge, do parser, filter, rewrite, or metadata stages explain the gap.

Fluentd exposes Prometheus-friendly counters such as fluentd_input_status_num_records_total on inputs and fluentd_output_status_emit_count on outputs when fluent-plugin-prometheus or equivalent agents scrape plugin metrics. Those counters advance monotonically in healthy fleets and should move together per tag when copy trees fan out predictably. fluentd_output_status_buffer_queue_length and buffer byte counters decouple visible ingress from visible egress when chunks stall inside the output buffer even though inputs still accept data: pair throughput parity with buffer analytics from UC-13.7.1 before you blame parsers alone. Parser helpers publish fluentd_parser_records_per_second_total style series when enabled; filter plugins publish fluentd_filter_records_per_second_total when instrumented. A sudden collapse in parser throughput relative to input totals often precedes regex mismatch storms or time format drift. rewrite_tag_filter and label-router style plugins can silently reroute events to unexpected match stanzas, which looks like loss for the original tag while another tag grows.

Fluent Bit publishes fluentbit_input_records_total and fluentbit_output_proc_records_total when Prometheus exporters or the HTTP monitoring interface summarize pipeline health. fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total highlights deliberate or emergent drops at filter boundaries, including Mem_Buf_Limit pressure and throttle policies. Splunk Connect for Kubernetes bundles opinionated Fluentd configurations with kubernetes_metadata filters, concat and multiline stages, and Splunk output stanzas: changes to default_namespace, container annotations, or deduplicate plugin settings routinely alter effective event counts even when raw container logs look unchanged. Calyptia builds may add enterprise filters with different defaults for drop_on_error semantics; treat vendor documentation as authoritative when reconciling counters.

RBAC and privacy: metrics and logs may include customer identifiers in tag names or Kubernetes labels. Restrict indexes, roles, and pager templates so only accountable teams see high-cardinality labels. Rotate Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens and scrub sample payloads during bridges. Legal review should confirm whether raw log lines attached to investigations may contain regulated data; prefer metric-first paging with hashed entity identifiers when jurisdictions require minimization.

Index design: land Prometheus scrape JSON into metrics indexes with explicit metric_name dimensions; land engine logs into index=fluentd with sourcetype=fluentd:logs; land Fluent Bit stderr into index=fluent_bit with sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs when you split namespaces. Maintain fluentd_pipeline_tag_inventory.csv keyed on tag with owner_team, sla_class, criticality, expected_steady_state_rate, runbook_url, and business_priority columns refreshed from service catalog exports. Maintain cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata with deduped tag rows for supplemental ownership when inventory lags during migrations.

Risk briefing: burst traffic from deploys can legitimately skew five-minute windows when batch sizes change. Scheduled log sampling or vendor compression experiments can change record counts without security incidents. Always correlate divergence with change_ticket_id metadata and maintenance calendars before customer-facing pages. Mis-tuned cardinality limits in Prometheus exporters can drop metric samples while logs continue, creating phantom parity gaps; monitor scrape success independently.

Differentiation recap: ingress versus egress counter parity per tag and output_id with parser and filter stage context, Fluent Bit input and filter-drop counters, optional engine log corroboration for pattern-not-match and warn drops, inventory and CMDB enrichment, not buffer-only saturation, not HTTP status-only rejection analytics, not TLS-only trust failures, not process crash loops in isolation.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms Splunk can fuse without assuming a single exporter tells the whole story. Arm one polls mstats for fluentd_input_status_num_records_total with span=5m aggregated by host and tag. Arm two polls mstats for fluentd_output_status_emit_count by host, tag, and output_id. Arm three polls mstats for fluentbit_input_records_total by input name and instance labels that map to Kubernetes nodes or pods. Arm four polls mstats for fluentd_parser_records_per_second_total and fluentd_filter_records_per_second_total together using metric_name IN filtering, grouped by host, tag, and plugin_id, to approximate parser and filter stage mass inside the same evaluation window. Arm five searches index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs for pattern not match, parser failures, dropped records, and bracketed warn lines that often accompany silent discards.

props.conf should preserve millisecond timestamps, generous TRUNCATE for stack-bearing lines, and LINE_BREAKER policies that keep multiline Ruby exceptions intact. transforms.conf should normalize kubernetes.pod_name into host only when host is empty to prevent duplicate entities. For Splunk Connect for Kubernetes, ensure helm values map the same tag labels into Prometheus dimensions that match inventory keys; mismatched label casing breaks joins and fabricates false divergence.

Pre-save validation: each arm must return rows within one hour in lab. Induce a harmless parser mismatch in non-production and confirm arms move together within two scrape intervals. Pause a lab output and confirm output_count_5min collapses while input_count_5min remains elevated.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

```bash
kubectl -n logging logs daemonset/splunk-connect-for-kubernetes-logging --tail=200
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:24220/api/plugins.json | head
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:2020/api/v1/metrics | head
```

Dashboards: dual-axis timechart of input_count_5min versus output_count_5min per tag, heatmap of throughput_diff_pct, and drilldowns to rewrite_tag_filter stanzas in configuration snapshots.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_3_fluent_throughput_parity with a five minute schedule during dense fleets, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate host, tag, output_id rows for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so incident commanders open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Fluentd input metrics, Fluentd output metrics, Fluent Bit input metrics, combined parser and filter metrics, and high-signal engine log buckets. bin span=5m aligns windows before stats. eventstats rebuilds pooled ingress totals per tag so each output_id row inherits the same input_count_5min baseline. streamstats window=2 approximates sustained ten minute elevation of throughput_diff_pct above five percent. eventstats latest output activity yields time_since_last_match staleness for silent outputs. join enriches owner_team and sla_class from fluentd_pipeline_tag_inventory.csv and cmdb fluentd_pipeline_metadata. case maps sustained divergence plus parser mass and staleness into critical, high, medium, and low tiers. coalesce absorbs missing host, tag, output_id, and owner_team keys from heterogeneous scrapers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.3 Fluentd Fluent Bit input-output throughput parity per tag output_id multisearch earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_input_status_num_records_total" span=5m BY host tag
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")))
      | eval output_id="aggregate_input"
      | eval lane="fluentd_input"
      | eval input_cnt=v
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=0
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_output_status_emit_count" span=5m BY host tag output_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")))
      | eval lane="fluentd_output"
      | eval input_cnt=0
      | eval output_cnt=v
      | eval parser_drop_hint=0
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentbit_input_records_total" span=5m BY name instance
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(name,"fluentbit_name")))
      | eval output_id="fluentbit_input_lane"
      | eval lane="fluentbit_input"
      | eval input_cnt=v
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=0
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name IN ("fluentd_parser_records_per_second_total","fluentd_filter_records_per_second_total") span=5m BY host tag plugin_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,"stage")))
      | eval lane="fluentd_stage"
      | eval input_cnt=0
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=round(v*300,0)
      | eval filter_in_v=v*300
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"pattern not match|failed to parse|parser|dropped|\\[warn\\]:|drop logs|chomp")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,kubernetes_pod_labels_log_stream,"unmapped_tag")))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS dc BY _time host tag
      | eval output_id="log_drop_evidence"
      | eval lane="fluentd_log"
      | eval input_cnt=0
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=dc
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(input_cnt) AS input_cnt_raw sum(output_cnt) AS output_cnt_raw sum(parser_drop_hint) AS parser_drop_raw sum(filter_in_v) AS filter_in_raw sum(filter_out_v) AS filter_out_raw BY _time host tag output_id
| eventstats sum(eval(if(output_id=="aggregate_input" OR output_id=="fluentbit_input_lane",input_cnt_raw,0))) AS pooled_in BY _time host tag
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval tag=coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")
| eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")
| eval input_count_5min=coalesce(pooled_in,0)
| eval output_count_5min=coalesce(output_cnt_raw,0)
| eval throughput_diff_pct=if(input_count_5min>0, round(100*abs(input_count_5min-output_count_5min)/input_count_5min,2), if(output_count_5min>0,100,0))
| eval parser_drop_count_5min=round(coalesce(parser_drop_raw,0),0)
| eval filter_chomp_pct=if(coalesce(filter_in_raw,0)>0, round(100*min(coalesce(parser_drop_count_5min,0),coalesce(filter_in_raw,0))/filter_in_raw,2), round(100*parser_drop_count_5min/nullif(input_count_5min,0),2))
| where NOT output_id IN ("aggregate_input","fluentbit_input_lane","log_drop_evidence")
| sort 0 host tag output_id + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(throughput_diff_pct>5,1,0))) AS sustain_gt5 BY host tag output_id
| eventstats latest(eval(if(output_count_5min>0,_time,null()))) AS last_out_time BY host tag output_id
| eval time_since_last_match=round((now()-coalesce(last_out_time,_time))/60,2)
| eventstats median(input_count_5min) AS env_in_med BY host
| join type=left max=0 tag
    [| inputlookup fluentd_pipeline_tag_inventory.csv
     | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,pipeline_tag)))
     | fields tag owner_team sla_class criticality expected_steady_state_rate runbook_url business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 tag
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup tag
     | eval tag=trim(toString(tag))
     | rename owner_team AS cmdb_owner
     | fields tag cmdb_owner pipeline_class ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(cmdb_owner,owner_team,"unowned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,pipeline_class,"standard")
| eval severity=case((sustain_gt5>=2 AND throughput_diff_pct>5 AND parser_drop_count_5min>100) OR time_since_last_match>5,"critical", throughput_diff_pct>5 OR parser_drop_count_5min>200,"high", throughput_diff_pct>2 OR parser_drop_count_5min>50,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND time_since_last_match>5,"restore_output_flush_confirm_hec_route_buffer_queue_and_copy_tree", severity=="critical","inspect_parser_filter_rewrite_tag_kubernetes_metadata_and_record_modifier", severity=="high","diff_config_against_sck_reference_validate_at_least_once_and_mem_buf_limits", true(),"continue_sampling_metrics_and_logs_for_tag_drift")
| table _time host tag output_id owner_team sla_class input_count_5min output_count_5min throughput_diff_pct parser_drop_count_5min filter_chomp_pct severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_3_fluent_throughput_parity_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_3_fluent_throughput_parity | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch imbalance, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on host, tag, and output_id, widen alert searches on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the shipper pod or host. Compare curl snapshots of monitor endpoints to Splunk mstats rows in the same five-minute bucket. Validate tag labels in Prometheus against live ConfigMaps before trusting divergence. For Splunk HTTP Event Collector routes, confirm at-least-once delivery settings and acknowledgment behavior match runbooks.

Synthetic validation: in lab, inject a regex that rejects most lines for one tag and confirm critical severity within two evaluation windows, then restore and confirm return to low.

Negative test: on healthy clusters after recovery, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds match your calibrated noise floor.

Runbook linkage: document expected drops during approved sampling windows so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show deliberate policy.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Upstream application changed log format without notice so parser regex updated in Git rejects ninety percent of lines: roll forward a tolerant staged parser, replay sampled lines through a lab forwarder, and validate fluentd_input_status_num_records_total still aligns with downstream emit counts before promoting.

Case 2 — rewrite_tag_filter routes events to a new tag after a Helm edit but the downstream match stanza never included that tag: restore the expected route map, compare against Splunk Connect for Kubernetes reference charts, and add integration tests that fail CI when orphan tags appear.

Case 3 — Kubernetes namespace label changes cause kubernetes_metadata filters to drop records that no longer match selector rules: reconcile namespace allowlists, patch ConfigMaps atomically, and verify pod annotations still carry required metadata fields.

Case 4 — Fluent Bit Mem_Buf_Limit is reached during a traffic spike so drop_log_events silently discards backlog: raise limits under vendor guidance, validate storage layers, and confirm fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total returns to baseline.

Case 5 — Operators disabled at_least_once_delivery semantics during an incident and forgot to restore them: re-enable durable delivery, validate buffer paths on disk, and document the temporary change with accountable sign-off.

Case 6 — Multi-line parser misconfiguration joins unrelated lines then discards oversized records: fix flush and continuation rules, validate containerd log rotation alignment, and replay canary workloads.

Case 7 — Fluentd v1.16 deprecation removed a plugin still referenced by an old ConfigMap: upgrade images uniformly, remove stale filters, and rebuild gem lockfiles under change control.

Case 8 — Splunk Connect for Kubernetes upgrade renamed default_namespace mapping so events carry unexpected index or source metadata that downstream routing drops: diff helm values between releases, restore explicit namespace keys, and validate HEC acknowledgements.

Case 9 — record_modifier strips fields required by Splunk index-time transforms so events appear to vanish in summaries while metrics still move: audit modifier rules, restore required keys, and validate schema tests.

Case 10 — HEC returns HTTP 200 with text=Invalid token in body while forwarders treat responses as success until counters stall: capture raw responses in debug lanes, rotate tokens, and validate load balancer health.

Case 11 — multi_format_parser falls through to ignore for a new JSON variant: add an explicit format clause, validate sample files, and monitor parser_drop_count_5min decay.

Case 12 — Circular rewrite_tag rules create loops that exhaust rewrite budgets and drop tail events: simplify routing graphs, remove reciprocal rewrites, and validate with dry-run tools where available.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, multisearch, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists thirteen analyst-facing columns including owner_team and sla_class aligned to inventory enrichment.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.3 Fluentd Fluent Bit input-output throughput parity per tag output_id multisearch earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_input_status_num_records_total" span=5m BY host tag
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")))
      | eval output_id="aggregate_input"
      | eval lane="fluentd_input"
      | eval input_cnt=v
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=0
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_output_status_emit_count" span=5m BY host tag output_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")))
      | eval lane="fluentd_output"
      | eval input_cnt=0
      | eval output_cnt=v
      | eval parser_drop_hint=0
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentbit_input_records_total" span=5m BY name instance
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(name,"fluentbit_name")))
      | eval output_id="fluentbit_input_lane"
      | eval lane="fluentbit_input"
      | eval input_cnt=v
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=0
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS v WHERE index=metrics metric_name IN ("fluentd_parser_records_per_second_total","fluentd_filter_records_per_second_total") span=5m BY host tag plugin_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,"stage")))
      | eval lane="fluentd_stage"
      | eval input_cnt=0
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=round(v*300,0)
      | eval filter_in_v=v*300
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"pattern not match|failed to parse|parser|dropped|\\[warn\\]:|drop logs|chomp")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,kubernetes_pod_labels_log_stream,"unmapped_tag")))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS dc BY _time host tag
      | eval output_id="log_drop_evidence"
      | eval lane="fluentd_log"
      | eval input_cnt=0
      | eval output_cnt=0
      | eval parser_drop_hint=dc
      | eval filter_in_v=0
      | eval filter_out_v=0
      | fields _time host tag output_id lane input_cnt output_cnt parser_drop_hint filter_in_v filter_out_v ]
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(input_cnt) AS input_cnt_raw sum(output_cnt) AS output_cnt_raw sum(parser_drop_hint) AS parser_drop_raw sum(filter_in_v) AS filter_in_raw sum(filter_out_v) AS filter_out_raw BY _time host tag output_id
| eventstats sum(eval(if(output_id=="aggregate_input" OR output_id=="fluentbit_input_lane",input_cnt_raw,0))) AS pooled_in BY _time host tag
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval tag=coalesce(tag,"unmapped_tag")
| eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")
| eval input_count_5min=coalesce(pooled_in,0)
| eval output_count_5min=coalesce(output_cnt_raw,0)
| eval throughput_diff_pct=if(input_count_5min>0, round(100*abs(input_count_5min-output_count_5min)/input_count_5min,2), if(output_count_5min>0,100,0))
| eval parser_drop_count_5min=round(coalesce(parser_drop_raw,0),0)
| eval filter_chomp_pct=if(coalesce(filter_in_raw,0)>0, round(100*min(coalesce(parser_drop_count_5min,0),coalesce(filter_in_raw,0))/filter_in_raw,2), round(100*parser_drop_count_5min/nullif(input_count_5min,0),2))
| where NOT output_id IN ("aggregate_input","fluentbit_input_lane","log_drop_evidence")
| sort 0 host tag output_id + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(throughput_diff_pct>5,1,0))) AS sustain_gt5 BY host tag output_id
| eventstats latest(eval(if(output_count_5min>0,_time,null()))) AS last_out_time BY host tag output_id
| eval time_since_last_match=round((now()-coalesce(last_out_time,_time))/60,2)
| eventstats median(input_count_5min) AS env_in_med BY host
| join type=left max=0 tag
    [| inputlookup fluentd_pipeline_tag_inventory.csv
     | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,pipeline_tag)))
     | fields tag owner_team sla_class criticality expected_steady_state_rate runbook_url business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 tag
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup tag
     | eval tag=trim(toString(tag))
     | rename owner_team AS cmdb_owner
     | fields tag cmdb_owner pipeline_class ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(cmdb_owner,owner_team,"unowned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,pipeline_class,"standard")
| eval severity=case((sustain_gt5>=2 AND throughput_diff_pct>5 AND parser_drop_count_5min>100) OR time_since_last_match>5,"critical", throughput_diff_pct>5 OR parser_drop_count_5min>200,"high", throughput_diff_pct>2 OR parser_drop_count_5min>50,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND time_since_last_match>5,"restore_output_flush_confirm_hec_route_buffer_queue_and_copy_tree", severity=="critical","inspect_parser_filter_rewrite_tag_kubernetes_metadata_and_record_modifier", severity=="high","diff_config_against_sck_reference_validate_at_least_once_and_mem_buf_limits", true(),"continue_sampling_metrics_and_logs_for_tag_drift")
| table _time host tag output_id owner_team sla_class input_count_5min output_count_5min throughput_diff_pct parser_drop_count_5min filter_chomp_pct severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.hostname) AS inv_host latest(Inventory.is_expected) AS is_expected FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.ip
| rename Inventory.ip AS shipper_ip
| join type=left max=0 shipper_ip [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS shipper_ip ]
| where is_expected=1 OR isnull(is_expected)
| table shipper_ip inv_host cpu_load mem_used
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by host, tag, and output_id with sparklines for input_count_5min, output_count_5min, and throughput_diff_pct; drilldowns to mstats series, fluentd:logs lines, fluent_bit:logs lines, inventory runbook_url, and buffer queue overlays.

## Known False Positives

Planned filter rollouts that intentionally drop noisy labels for a subset of tags elevate parser_drop_count_5min without customer impact when change_ticket metadata and canary namespaces are present; require owner acknowledgement before paging application teams. Controlled drop policies for non-critical noise tags during cost governance windows change throughput parity by design; gate alerts using business_priority and environment labels rather than muting entire clusters. Scheduled compression sampling or vendor-side cardinality controls can reduce emitted records while upstream volume remains high; pair with vendor communications before declaring shipper failure. Deliberate developer-cluster drop policies and throttled lab tokens produce chronic divergence that should never route like production; downgrade using inventory criticality columns. Vendor-announced silent-drop maintenance for hosted observability endpoints can stall emits while inputs advance; correlate with status pages and maintenance calendars. Fluent Bit DaemonSet restarts during node drains create short windows where instance labels shift and mstats arms briefly disagree; confirm pod continuity before rewriting configuration. Mis-labeled tag keys in Prometheus after a helm refactor fabricate orphan inventory joins; refresh fluentd_pipeline_tag_inventory.csv weekly and validate tag normalization. Duplicate scrapers that double-count fluentd_input_status_num_records_total inflate input_count_5min until dedupe summaries land; validate exporter cardinality rules. Replay jobs that intentionally re-ingest historical logs can make current-window parity look broken when outputs deduplicate at the sink; require replay identifiers in annotations. Approved chaos experiments that pause outputs in non-production namespaces should fire only inside labeled environments.
